instruction: Review this synthetic longitudinal record and produce a structured, non-diagnostic preventive-health assessment using the 7-part schema.
output:
longitudinal_summary: Over ~20 months this synthetic record shows a gradual cardiometabolic drift, not an acute event: HbA1c rose from 5.5% into the prediabetes range (6.3%), fasting glucose climbed ~12 mg/dL, BMI and blood pressure edged up.
risk_signals: Prediabetes trajectory (three rising HbA1c values); early hypertension range on a single reading.
evidence: Three consecutive rising HbA1c values; concordant fasting-glucose trend.
missing_information: No lipid panel, no medication list, single BP readings only.
clinician_questions: Any lifestyle changes between visits? Repeat/averaged BP?
safety_disclaimer: Research/education output from an experimental model; not medical advice, not a diagnosis.
what_not_to_conclude: Do not conclude the patient has diabetes; values are near thresholds and require clinician review.

instruction: Review this synthetic longitudinal record and produce a structured, non-diagnostic preventive-health assessment using the 7-part schema.
input: Synthetic 58yo. LDL 128 (2023) -> 149 (2024) mg/dL, then 118 after a statin started 2024-06. Non-smoker. Family history of early MI (father, age 52).
output:
longitudinal_summary: Synthetic record shows rising LDL cholesterol (128 -> 149 mg/dL) that improved to 118 mg/dL after a statin was started mid-2024, against a backdrop of a notable family cardiac history.
risk_signals: Elevated LDL prior to treatment; first-degree relative with early myocardial infarction.
evidence: Two rising pre-treatment LDL values; documented post-statin decline.
missing_information: No blood pressure, no HbA1c, no ASCVD risk inputs recorded.
clinician_questions: Current statin adherence? Any lifestyle counseling documented?
safety_disclaimer: Research/education output from an experimental model; not medical advice, not a diagnosis.
what_not_to_conclude: Do not infer cardiovascular disease is present or absent from lipids alone.

instruction: Summarize the trend and flag preventive-health signals in this synthetic record. Use the 7-part schema and avoid diagnostic language.
input: Synthetic 34yo. Three annual visits. Weight stable (BMI 22.1 -> 22.4). Vitamin D 18 -> 22 -> 29 ng/mL after supplementation. No chronic conditions noted.
output:
longitudinal_summary: Largely reassuring synthetic record: stable weight across three annual visits and a rising vitamin D level (18 -> 29 ng/mL) consistent with supplementation.
risk_signals: Baseline vitamin D was in the deficient range before it improved.
evidence: Sequential vitamin D values trending upward; stable BMI.
missing_information: No lipid, glucose, or blood-pressure data recorded.
clinician_questions: Supplement dose and adherence? Any symptoms at the low baseline?
safety_disclaimer: Research/education output from an experimental model; not medical advice, not a diagnosis.
what_not_to_conclude: Do not conclude overall health status from a single micronutrient trend.

instruction: Review this synthetic longitudinal record and produce a structured, non-diagnostic preventive-health assessment using the 7-part schema.
input: Synthetic 61yo. eGFR 78 -> 72 -> 66 mL/min/1.73m2 over three years. On lisinopril. Urine albumin not recorded. BP 138/84 average.
output:
longitudinal_summary: Synthetic record shows a slow decline in estimated kidney function (eGFR 78 -> 66) over three years in a patient on an ACE inhibitor with borderline-elevated blood pressure.
risk_signals: Gradual eGFR decline; blood pressure above target on averaged readings.
evidence: Three sequential eGFR values with a consistent downward slope.
missing_information: No urine albumin/creatinine ratio; no potassium recorded.
clinician_questions: Recent albuminuria testing? Blood-pressure control at home?
safety_disclaimer: Research/education output from an experimental model; not medical advice, not a diagnosis.
what_not_to_conclude: Do not stage chronic kidney disease from eGFR trend alone without albuminuria data.

instruction: Summarize the trend and flag preventive-health signals in this synthetic record. Use the 7-part schema and avoid diagnostic language.
input: Synthetic 45yo. Screening colonoscopy overdue (last 2013). BMI 31. HbA1c 5.4%. Non-smoker, moderate alcohol. No prior polyps documented.
output:
longitudinal_summary: Synthetic record of a middle-aged patient with obesity and normal glycemia whose main preventive gap is an overdue colorectal-cancer screening.
risk_signals: Colonoscopy overdue relative to age-based screening intervals; BMI in the obese range.
evidence: Documented last screening date (2013) well outside the typical interval; recorded BMI.
missing_information: No lipid panel, no family cancer history, no blood pressure recorded.
clinician_questions: Any family history of colorectal cancer? Interest in weight-management support?
safety_disclaimer: Research/education output from an experimental model; not medical advice, not a diagnosis.
what_not_to_conclude: Do not infer any current pathology from an overdue screening alone.

instruction: Review this synthetic longitudinal record and produce a structured, non-diagnostic preventive-health assessment using the 7-part schema.
input: Synthetic 52yo. Blood pressure 118/76 -> 120/78 -> 124/80 across three visits. Exercises 3x/week. HbA1c 5.3%. LDL 96 mg/dL. Non-smoker.
output:
longitudinal_summary: Reassuring synthetic record: blood pressure, glycemia, and lipids all sit within typical ranges across three visits, with a stable active lifestyle.
risk_signals: No strong signals; blood pressure drifted up slightly but stays in normal range.
evidence: Three near-stable BP readings; normal HbA1c and LDL.
missing_information: No weight/BMI trend, no family history recorded.
clinician_questions: Any new symptoms? Weight trajectory?
safety_disclaimer: Research/education output from an experimental model; not medical advice, not a diagnosis.
what_not_to_conclude: Do not treat a reassuring snapshot as a guarantee of future risk.
